scope_note: QA CM Relative Norm and Tower Law cert [397]. Primary sources: Neukirch (1999) 'Algebraic Number Theory' ISBN 978-3-540-65399-8 §II.10 (CM fields as totally imaginary quadratic extensions of totally real fields; Hecke characters; relative norm tower; N_{K/Q} = N_{F/Q} ∘ N_{K/F}); Washington (1997) 'Introduction to Cyclotomic Fields' ISBN 978-0-387-94762-4 Ch.1 (Z[zeta_5] as CM ring, complex conjugation sigma_K: zeta_5->zeta_5^4, Z[phi] as max totally real suborder); Lang (1994) 'Algebraic Number Theory' ISBN 978-1-4612-0853-2 §VIII.2 (tower formula for norms and traces, minimal polynomial over intermediate field); Silverman (1994) 'Advanced Topics in the Arithmetic of Elliptic Curves' ISBN 978-0-387-94328-2 §II.10 (CM theory for abelian varieties, Hecke Grossencharacter, CM Frobenius eigenvalue as trace of Hecke character). CLAIM (narrow, falsifiable): For pi = a+b*zeta_5 in Z[zeta_5] with N_{K/Q}(pi) = p (rational prime, p ≡ 1 mod 5), K=Q(zeta_5), F=Q(sqrt(5)): (C1) N_{K/F}(a+b*zeta_5) = (a^2-ab+b^2)+ab*phi in Z[phi] (exact integer formula); (C2) N_{F/Q}(N_{K/F}(pi)) = N_{K/Q}(pi) = p (tower law, algebraic identity verified for 4 primes); (C3) N_{K/F}(pi)*sigma_F(N_{K/F}(pi)) = p (CM norm factoring, N_{K/F} and sigma_F(N_{K/F}) are the two F/Q-conjugate halves of p); (C4) CM minimal polynomial of pi over F is X^2 - Tr_{K/F}(pi)*X + N_{K/F}(pi) with Tr_{K/F}=(2a-b)+b*phi (= partial trace from cert [396]) and N_{K/F}=(a^2-ab+b^2)+ab*phi; (C5) CM discriminant disc=Tr^2-4*N_rel=-b^2*(2+phi) is TOTALLY NEGATIVE (both components negative, N_{F/Q}(disc)=5*b^4>0), which implies the CM min poly has no real roots in either real embedding of F — this is the degree-4/CM analog of the Weil discriminant sign flip in cert [395]. Algebraic identity proof (C2): let u=a^2-ab+b^2, v=ab; then u^2+uv-v^2 = (a^2-ab+b^2)^2+(a^2-ab+b^2)(ab)-(ab)^2 = a^4-a^3b+a^2b^2-ab^3+b^4. Totally negative discriminant proof (C5): disc=-b^2*(2+phi); 2+phi≈3.618>0 and 2+(1-phi)=3-phi≈1.382>0, so both real embeddings of -b^2*(2+phi) are negative. Theorem NT: Z[phi] is represented as integer pairs (u,v); all operations are exact integer arithmetic (zphi_add, zphi_mul, sigma_F, zphi_norm); no floats enter the QA layer. Comparison table: cert [395] checks a_f^2-4p<0 at one real place (over Q); cert [397] checks disc=-b^2*(2+phi)<<0 at both real places of F=Q(sqrt(5)) (totally negative); both are equivalent to Weil bound but at different Langlands levels. Extends cert [396] (Z[zeta_5] infrastructure, C^5=I, partial trace formula which appears here as Tr_{K/F}). The partial trace from cert [396] is identified as the CM Hecke eigenvalue candidate pi+sigma_K(pi). 5 checks PASS; self-test ok. Derived 2026-06-12.
